sentences.quote: We present the first formalization of the Witten-Reshetikhin-Turaev 3-manifold invariant pipeline in any proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: we present the first formalization of the witten-reshetikhin-turaev 3-manifold invariant pipeline in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: Meta-claim of priority/novelty. Not mechanically verifiable; survey-level claim about prior art absence. No structural drift detectable from pipeline state.

meta.quote_normalized: we present the first formalization of the witten-reshetikhin-turaev 3-manifold invariant pipeline in any proof assistant.

sentences.quote: Starting from verified modular tensor category data (Ising and Fibonacci MTCs with machine-checked R-matrices, F-symbols, pentagon, hexagon, and ribbon conditions), we build a complete chain through the Temperley-Lieb algebra, Jones-Wenzl idempotents, surgery presentations for 3-manifolds, to the WRT surgery formula connecting topological data to algebraic invariants.
sentences.quote_normalized: starting from verified modular tensor category data (ising and fibonacci mtcs with machine-checked r-matrices, f-symbols, pentagon, hexagon, and ribbon conditions), we build a complete chain through the temperley-lieb algebra, jones-wenzl idempotents, surgery presentations for 3-manifolds, to the wrt surgery formula connecting topological data to algebraic invariants.
sentences.agent_notes: Methodology summary. Pipeline modules TemperleyLieb, JonesWenzl, SurgeryPresentation, WRTInvariant, WRTComputation all exist with theorems present in lean_deps.json.

meta.quote_normalized: starting from verified modular tensor category data (ising and fibonacci mtcs with machine-checked r-matrices, f-symbols, pentagon, hexagon, and ribbon conditions), we build a complete chain through the temperley-lieb algebra, jones-wenzl idempotents, surgery presentations for 3-manifolds, to the wrt surgery formula connecting topological data to algebraic invariants.

sentences.quote: Concrete computations verified by \nativedecide{}: Ising $D^2 = 4$, Gauss sum $p_+ = 2\zeta_{16}$; Fibonacci $D^2 = 2 + \varphi$ with $\varphi^2 = \varphi + 1$.
sentences.quote_normalized: concrete computations verified by \nativedecide{}: ising $d^2 = 4$, gauss sum $p_+ = 2\zeta_{16}$; fibonacci $d^2 = 2 + \varphi$ with $\varphi^2 = \varphi + 1$.
sentences.agent_notes: Concrete numeric claims about D² and p_+ for Ising / D² for Fibonacci. WRTComputation module hosts native_decide-verified Ising and Fibonacci computations. Phi-quadratic relation phi^2 = phi+1 is the defining minimal polynomial; algebraically routine. Ising D^2 = 1 + 2 + 1 = 4 is correct (sigma has dimension sqrt 2). Fibonacci D^2 = 1 + phi^2 = 2 + phi is correct.

meta.quote_normalized: concrete computations verified by \nativedecide{}: ising $d^2 = 4$, gauss sum $p_+ = 2\zeta_{16}$; fibonacci $d^2 = 2 + \varphi$ with $\varphi^2 = \varphi + 1$.

sentences.quote: The Temperley-Lieb algebra (3~relation types), Jones-Wenzl recurrence, surgery presentations ($S^3$, $S^2 \times S^1$, lens spaces), and the WRT formula are all first formalizations in any proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: the temperley-lieb algebra (3~relation types), jones-wenzl recurrence, surgery presentations ($s^3$, $s^2 \times s^1$, lens spaces), and the wrt formula are all first formalizations in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: Three relations match TL1/TL2/TL3 in TemperleyLieb.lean. Priority claim ('first formalizations') is a survey-level meta-claim, not mechanically verifiable here.

meta.quote_normalized: the temperley-lieb algebra (3~relation types), jones-wenzl recurrence, surgery presentations ($s^3$, $s^2 \times s^1$, lens spaces), and the wrt formula are all first formalizations in any proof assistant.

sentences.quote: The Witten-Reshetikhin-Turaev invariant~\cite{witten1989,rt1991} assigns a complex number $Z(M)$ to each closed oriented 3-manifold $M$, computed from a modular tensor category (MTC).
sentences.quote_normalized: the witten-reshetikhin-turaev invariant~ assigns a complex number $z(m)$ to each closed oriented 3-manifold $m$, computed from a modular tensor category (mtc).
sentences.agent_notes: Citation keys 'witten1989' and 'rt1991' are not present in CITATION_REGISTRY (src/core/citations.py). Bibitems exist in paper_draft.tex thebibliography (lines 194-198). Definitional statement about WRT invariant is standard mathematical content. WARN on registry coverage gap; not FAIL because paper bibitems resolve in-document.

meta.quote_normalized: the witten-reshetikhin-turaev invariant~ assigns a complex number $z(m)$ to each closed oriented 3-manifold $m$, computed from a modular tensor category (mtc).

sentences.quote: Despite 35~years of development and central importance in quantum topology, no component of the WRT construction has previously been formalized in any proof assistant --- not the surgery formula, not the Temperley-Lieb algebra, not the Kirby calculus, and not any quantum 3-manifold invariant value.
sentences.quote_normalized: despite 35~years of development and central importance in quantum topology, no component of the wrt construction has previously been formalized in any proof assistant --- not the surgery formula, not the temperley-lieb algebra, not the kirby calculus, and not any quantum 3-manifold invariant value.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority/novelty meta-claim about prior art. Not mechanically verifiable from pipeline state.

meta.quote_normalized: despite 35~years of development and central importance in quantum topology, no component of the wrt construction has previously been formalized in any proof assistant --- not the surgery formula, not the temperley-lieb algebra, not the kirby calculus, and not any quantum 3-manifold invariant value.

sentences.quote: We close this gap with a complete formalization pipeline in Lean~4 with Mathlib, building on our prior work establishing the first verified MTC instances (Ising and Fibonacci) with machine-checked pentagon, hexagon, and ribbon coherence~\cite{paper14}.
sentences.quote_normalized: we close this gap with a complete formalization pipeline in lean~4 with mathlib, building on our prior work establishing the first verified mtc instances (ising and fibonacci) with machine-checked pentagon, hexagon, and ribbon coherence~.
sentences.agent_notes: Citation 'paper14' is a self-reference to a companion paper; not in CITATION_REGISTRY. Bibitem present in document.

meta.quote_normalized: we close this gap with a complete formalization pipeline in lean~4 with mathlib, building on our prior work establishing the first verified mtc instances (ising and fibonacci) with machine-checked pentagon, hexagon, and ribbon coherence~.

sentences.quote: The pipeline comprises seven new modules totaling ${\sim}60$~theorems with zero \texttt{sorry} and zero axioms.
sentences.quote_normalized: the pipeline comprises seven new modules totaling ${\sim}60$~theorems with zero sorry and zero axioms.

Author's '~60' undercounts current state by ~30-50%. Approximation tilde tolerates some drift but this is large; WARN. Sorry/axiom counts confirmed zero.

sentences.quote: The Temperley-Lieb algebra $\mathrm{TL}_n(\delta)$ is the algebraic foundation of the BHMV construction~\cite{bhmv1995} of the WRT TQFT functor.
sentences.quote_normalized: the temperley-lieb algebra $tl_n(\delta)$ is the algebraic foundation of the bhmv construction~ of the wrt tqft functor.
sentences.agent_notes: Citation 'bhmv1995' not in CITATION_REGISTRY; bibitem present in document. Definitional/historical claim.

meta.quote_normalized: the temperley-lieb algebra $tl_n(\delta)$ is the algebraic foundation of the bhmv construction~ of the wrt tqft functor.

sentences.quote: We define it in \lean{TemperleyLieb.lean} as a quotient of the free algebra on $n$ generators $\{e_0, \ldots, e_{n-1}\}$ by three relation families:
sentences.quote_normalized: we define it in \lean{temperleylieb.lean} as a quotient of the free algebra on $n$ generators $\{e_0, \ldots, e_{n-1}\}$ by three relation families:
sentences.agent_notes: Module SKEFTHawking.TemperleyLieb exists (lean/SKEFTHawking/TemperleyLieb.lean); 4 theorems in lean_deps.json under module=SKEFTHawking.TemperleyLieb.

meta.quote_normalized: we define it in \lean{temperleylieb.lean} as a quotient of the free algebra on $n$ generators $\{e_0, \ldots, e_{n-1}\}$ by three relation families:

sentences.quote: \begin{align} \text{(TL1)} && e_i^2 &= \delta \cdot e_i \label{eq:tl1} \\ \text{(TL2)} && e_i e_j e_i &= e_i \quad (|i-j|=1) \label{eq:tl2} \\ \text{(TL3)} && e_i e_j &= e_j e_i \quad (|i-j| \ge 2) \label{eq:tl3} \end{align}
sentences.quote_normalized: \begin{align} (tl1) && e_i^2 &= \delta \cdot e_i \\ (tl2) && e_i e_j e_i &= e_i \quad (|i-j|=1) \\ (tl3) && e_i e_j &= e_j e_i \quad (|i-j| \ge 2) \end{align}
sentences.agent_notes: Three Temperley-Lieb relation families. Three relation types match TL1/TL2/TL3 in TemperleyLieb.lean. Standard TL presentation in Wenzl/BHMV literature.

meta.quote_normalized: \begin{align} (tl1) && e_i^2 &= \delta \cdot e_i \\ (tl2) && e_i e_j e_i &= e_i \quad (|i-j|=1) \\ (tl3) && e_i e_j &= e_j e_i \quad (|i-j| \ge 2) \end{align}

sentences.quote: All three relations are proved via the standard \lean{RingQuot.mkAlgHom\_rel} descent from the free algebra.
sentences.quote_normalized: all three relations are proved via the standard \lean{ringquot.mkalghom\_rel} descent from the free algebra.
sentences.agent_notes: RingQuot.mkAlgHom_rel is a Mathlib lemma; not in lean_deps.json (which scans SKEFTHawking only). Standard Mathlib API for RingQuot descent — TN does not apply to non-SK-EFT identifiers.

meta.quote_normalized: all three relations are proved via the standard \lean{ringquot.mkalghom\_rel} descent from the free algebra.

sentences.quote: The parameter $\delta$ is the loop value; for SU(2)$_k$ quantum groups, $\delta = [2]_q = q + q^{-1}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the parameter $\delta$ is the loop value; for su(2)$_k$ quantum groups, $\delta = [2]_q = q + q^{-1}$.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard q-number identity [2]_q = q + q^-1; expository definition.

meta.quote_normalized: the parameter $\delta$ is the loop value; for su(2)$_k$ quantum groups, $\delta = [2]_q = q + q^{-1}$.

sentences.quote: The Jones-Wenzl idempotent $f^{(m)} \in \mathrm{TL}_n$ is defined by the Wenzl recurrence~\cite{wenzl1988}: \begin{equation} f^{(0)} = 1, \quad f^{(m+1)} = f^{(m)} - c(m) \cdot f^{(m)} \cdot e_m \cdot f^{(m)} \end{equation}
sentences.quote_normalized: the jones-wenzl idempotent $f^{(m)} \in tl_n$ is defined by the wenzl recurrence~: \begin{equation} f^{(0)} = 1, \quad f^{(m+1)} = f^{(m)} - c(m) \cdot f^{(m)} \cdot e_m \cdot f^{(m)} \end{equation}
sentences.agent_notes: Citation 'wenzl1988' not in CITATION_REGISTRY; bibitem present. JonesWenzl module exists with 4 theorems (grep)/8 (lean_deps).

meta.quote_normalized: the jones-wenzl idempotent $f^{(m)} \in tl_n$ is defined by the wenzl recurrence~: \begin{equation} f^{(0)} = 1, \quad f^{(m+1)} = f^{(m)} - c(m) \cdot f^{(m)} \cdot e_m \cdot f^{(m)} \end{equation}

sentences.quote: where $c(m) = [m]_q / [m+1]_q$ for the quantum-group specialization.
sentences.quote_normalized: where $c(m) = [m]_q / [m+1]_q$ for the quantum-group specialization.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard Jones-Wenzl coefficient. Expository formula.

meta.quote_normalized: where $c(m) = [m]_q / [m+1]_q$ for the quantum-group specialization.

sentences.quote: We parametrize by an abstract coefficient function $c : \mathbb{N} \to k$, keeping the definition valid over any commutative ring.

sentences.quote: We define \lean{MTCWithTwist} extending \lean{PreModularData} with topological twist eigenvalues $\theta_i$, global dimension $D^2 = \sum_i d_i^2$, and Gauss sum $p_+ = \sum_i d_i^2 \theta_i$.

sentences.agent_notes: Both names exist: SKEFTHawking.MTCWithTwist (WRTInvariant.lean line 47) extends SKEFTHawking.PreModularData (RibbonCategory.lean line 85). Definitions of globalDimSq and gaussPlus appear at WRTInvariant.lean lines 54 and 58.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Ising MTC} (SU(2)$_2$, 3 simple objects $\{1, \sigma, \psi\}$):
sentences.quote_normalized: ising mtc (su(2)$_2$, 3 simple objects $\{1, \sigma, \psi\}$):
sentences.agent_notes: Section header for Ising MTC results; descriptive labelling.

sentences.quote: $D^2 = 1 + (\sqrt{2})^2 + 1 = 4$ \quad (\nativedecide{} over $\mathbb{Q}(\zeta_{16})$)
sentences.quote_normalized: $d^2 = 1 + (\sqrt{2})^2 + 1 = 4$ \quad (\nativedecide{} over $\mathbb{q}(\zeta_{16})$)
sentences.agent_notes: Ising D^2 = 1 + 2 + 1 = 4. Quantum dimensions for Ising: d_1=1, d_sigma=sqrt(2), d_psi=1. Sum of squares = 1+2+1 = 4. Exact arithmetic match.

meta.quote_normalized: $d^2 = 1 + (\sqrt{2})^2 + 1 = 4$ \quad (\nativedecide{} over $\mathbb{q}(\zeta_{16})$)

sentences.quote: $p_+ = 1 + 2\theta_\sigma + \theta_\psi = 2\zeta_{16}$ \quad (proved)
sentences.quote_normalized: $p_+ = 1 + 2\theta_\sigma + \theta_\psi = 2\zeta_{16}$ \quad (proved)
sentences.agent_notes: Ising Gauss sum p_+ = sum_i d_i^2 theta_i. With theta_sigma = zeta_16, theta_psi = -1: p_+ = 1·1 + 2·zeta_16 + 1·(-1) = 2·zeta_16. Algebraically consistent.

meta.quote_normalized: $p_+ = 1 + 2\theta_\sigma + \theta_\psi = 2\zeta_{16}$ \quad (proved)

sentences.quote: $Z(S^2 \times S^1) = 3$ \quad (rank)
sentences.quote_normalized: $z(s^2 \times s^1) = 3$ \quad (rank)
sentences.agent_notes: Ising has 3 simple objects → rank = 3 → Z(S²×S¹) = 3. Matches wrt_S2xS1_eq_rank.

meta.quote_normalized: $z(s^2 \times s^1) = 3$ \quad (rank)

sentences.agent_notes: Ising braiding generator on the 2-anyon space. IsingBraiding module has 25 theorems formalizing this; Clifford-group property is standard for Ising MTC.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Lens space invariants} $Z(L(p,1))$ for $p = 1,2,3,4,8,16$: numerator $= 1 + 2\theta_\sigma^p + (-1)^p$, all proved.
sentences.quote_normalized: lens space invariants $z(l(p,1))$ for $p = 1,2,3,4,8,16$: numerator $= 1 + 2\theta_\sigma^p + (-1)^p$, all proved.
sentences.agent_notes: WRT lens-space surgery formula numerator = sum_i d_i^2 theta_i^p (for p-surgery). With Ising d_i=1,sqrt2,1 and theta_psi=-1, theta_1=1: numerator = 1·1 + 2·theta_sigma^p + 1·(-1)^p. Matches.

meta.quote_normalized: lens space invariants $z(l(p,1))$ for $p = 1,2,3,4,8,16$: numerator $= 1 + 2\theta_\sigma^p + (-1)^p$, all proved.

sentences.quote: Key: $Z(L(8,1)) = 0$ (vanishing!) and $Z(L(16,1)) = D^2/D^2 = 1$ (trivial), encoding the 16-fold periodicity at the 3-manifold level.
sentences.quote_normalized: key: $z(l(8,1)) = 0$ (vanishing!) and $z(l(16,1)) = d^2/d^2 = 1$ (trivial), encoding the 16-fold periodicity at the 3-manifold level.
sentences.agent_notes: With theta_sigma = zeta_16 (primitive 16th root): theta_sigma^8 = -1, so numerator at p=8 is 1 + 2·(-1) + 1 = 0. At p=16: theta_sigma^16 = 1, numerator = 1 + 2 + 1 = 4 = D^2; ratio = 1. 16-fold periodicity follows from theta_sigma being a primitive 16th root.

meta.quote_normalized: key: $z(l(8,1)) = 0$ (vanishing!) and $z(l(16,1)) = d^2/d^2 = 1$ (trivial), encoding the 16-fold periodicity at the 3-manifold level.

sentences.quote: The q-binomial coefficient $\binom{n}{j}_q$ is defined via the Laurent Pascal recurrence without division (\lean{QNumber.lean}): \begin{equation} \binom{n{+}1}{j{+}1}_q = q^{j+1} \binom{n}{j{+}1}_q + q^{j-n} \binom{n}{j}_q \end{equation}
sentences.quote_normalized: the q-binomial coefficient $\binom{n}{j}_q$ is defined via the laurent pascal recurrence without division (\lean{qnumber.lean}): \begin{equation} \binom{n{+}1}{j{+}1}_q = q^{j+1} \binom{n}{j{+}1}_q + q^{j-n} \binom{n}{j}_q \end{equation}
sentences.agent_notes: QNumber.lean module exists with 20-25 theorems; Laurent-Pascal recurrence is one standard formulation of q-binomial.

meta.quote_normalized: the q-binomial coefficient $\binom{n}{j}_q$ is defined via the laurent pascal recurrence without division (\lean{qnumber.lean}): \begin{equation} \binom{n{+}1}{j{+}1}_q = q^{j+1} \binom{n}{j{+}1}_q + q^{j-n} \binom{n}{j}_q \end{equation}

sentences.quote: with classical limit $\binom{n}{j}_1 = C(n,j)$ proved by induction.
sentences.quote_normalized: with classical limit $\binom{n}{j}_1 = c(n,j)$ proved by induction.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard classical-limit identity; QNumber.lean covers q-binomial properties.

meta.quote_normalized: with classical limit $\binom{n}{j}_1 = c(n,j)$ proved by induction.

sentences.quote: Key values: $\binom{2}{1}_q = [2]_q$ (simply-laced Serre coefficient), $\binom{3}{1}_q = [3]_q$ (for $B_n/C_n$ types).
sentences.quote_normalized: key values: $\binom{2}{1}_q = [2]_q$ (simply-laced serre coefficient), $\binom{3}{1}_q = [3]_q$ (for $b_n/c_n$ types).
sentences.agent_notes: Standard q-binomial values: C(2,1)_q = [2]_q, C(3,1)_q = [3]_q. Matches Serre relation coefficients in simply-laced and B_n/C_n type quantum groups.

meta.quote_normalized: key values: $\binom{2}{1}_q = [2]_q$ (simply-laced serre coefficient), $\binom{3}{1}_q = [3]_q$ (for $b_n/c_n$ types).

sentences.quote: We have presented the first formalization of the WRT 3-manifold invariant pipeline: from algebraic MTC data through the Temperley-Lieb algebra, Jones-Wenzl idempotents, surgery presentations with Kirby moves, to concrete verified invariant values and anyonic braiding gates.
sentences.quote_normalized: we have presented the first formalization of the wrt 3-manifold invariant pipeline: from algebraic mtc data through the temperley-lieb algebra, jones-wenzl idempotents, surgery presentations with kirby moves, to concrete verified invariant values and anyonic braiding gates.
sentences.agent_notes: Closing meta-claim about novelty/scope. Not mechanically verifiable.

meta.quote_normalized: we have presented the first formalization of the wrt 3-manifold invariant pipeline: from algebraic mtc data through the temperley-lieb algebra, jones-wenzl idempotents, surgery presentations with kirby moves, to concrete verified invariant values and anyonic braiding gates.

sentences.quote: Nine Lean~4 modules, ${\sim}115$~theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms.
sentences.quote_normalized: nine lean~4 modules, ${\sim}115$~theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: Conclusion claims '9 modules, ~115 theorems'. Abstract says '7 modules' (line 33). Aggregate over plausible 9-module set (TL+JW+Surgery+WRTInv+WRTComp+QNumber+IsingBraiding+IsingGates+FibonacciBraiding) by `^theorem ` grep: 4+4+20+3+30+20+25+21+33 = 160. Alternate scope (TL+JW+Surgery+WRTInv+WRTComp+QNumber+IsingBraiding+FibQutrit+FibQutritUniv) = 122. Either way '~115' undercounts current state. Live counts via lean_deps.json kind=theorem: aggregate ~250+ across the 9 plausible modules. WARN: ~ tilde gives loose tolerance but discrepancy is large. Sorry/axiom counts confirmed zero for all WRT modules.

meta.quote_normalized: nine lean~4 modules, ${\sim}115$~theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms.

sentences.quote: The Ising and Fibonacci computations demonstrate that verified MTC data can produce both verified topological invariants and verified quantum gates --- the first such connections in any proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: the ising and fibonacci computations demonstrate that verified mtc data can produce both verified topological invariants and verified quantum gates --- the first such connections in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority/significance meta-claim; not mechanically verifiable.

meta.quote_normalized: the ising and fibonacci computations demonstrate that verified mtc data can produce both verified topological invariants and verified quantum gates --- the first such connections in any proof assistant.

sentences.quote: Braiding gates computed via $B = F^{-1}RF$: Ising gates generate the Clifford group, Fibonacci gates generate a dense subgroup of SU(2) (Freedman--Larsen--Wang 2002).
sentences.quote_normalized: braiding gates computed via $b = f^{-1}rf$: ising gates generate the clifford group, fibonacci gates generate a dense subgroup of su(2) (freedman--larsen--wang 2002).
sentences.agent_notes: Cites Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002 as inline reference (no \cite — 'Freedman--Larsen--Wang 2002' as plain text). Citation is real (Freedman, Larsen, Wang, Comm. Math. Phys. 227, 605 (2002)) but not in CITATION_REGISTRY nor in thebibliography. Standard Fibonacci anyon universality result. Note: a strict citation_integrity gate may flag this inline reference; INFO here since substance is correct.

meta.quote_normalized: braiding gates computed via $b = f^{-1}rf$: ising gates generate the clifford group, fibonacci gates generate a dense subgroup of su(2) (freedman--larsen--wang 2002).

sentences.quote: A self-contained universality proof via Lie algebra spanning avoids the classification of finite simple groups: the commutator $[\sigma_1, \sigma_2]$ is nonzero and structurally independent from the generators, spanning $\mathfrak{su}(2)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: a self-contained universality proof via lie algebra spanning avoids the classification of finite simple groups: the commutator $[\sigma_1, \sigma_2]$ is nonzero and structurally independent from the generators, spanning $\mathfrak{su}(2)$.
sentences.agent_notes: FibonacciUniversality.lean (9 theorems) hosts Lie-algebra-spanning universality proof. su(2) spanning matches Fibonacci structure.

meta.quote_normalized: a self-contained universality proof via lie algebra spanning avoids the classification of finite simple groups: the commutator $[\sigma_1, \sigma_2]$ is nonzero and structurally independent from the generators, spanning $\mathfrak{su}(2)$.

sentences.quote: The full braiding data requires the non-cyclotomic degree-8 field $K = \mathbb{Q}(\zeta_5, \sqrt{\varphi})$, implemented as 8-tuples of rationals with verified $w^2 = \varphi$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the full braiding data requires the non-cyclotomic degree-8 field $k = \mathbb{q}(\zeta_5, \sqrt{\varphi})$, implemented as 8-tuples of rationals with verified $w^2 = \varphi$.
sentences.agent_notes: Q(zeta_5, sqrt(phi)) is degree 4·2 = 8 over Q. FibonacciBraiding (33 thms) and FibonacciQutrit (6 thms) host the 8-tuple field implementation; w^2 = phi is the defining relation for the sqrt(phi) extension.

meta.quote_normalized: the full braiding data requires the non-cyclotomic degree-8 field $k = \mathbb{q}(\zeta_5, \sqrt{\varphi})$, implemented as 8-tuples of rationals with verified $w^2 = \varphi$.

sentences.quote: For 4~anyons on the qutrit space (dim~3), all $B_4$ relations verified as $3 \times 3$ matrix identities over $K$, with $\sigma_1 \neq \sigma_3$ (genuine $B_4$, not factoring through $B_3$) and su(3) Lie algebra spanning data establishing density in SU(3).
sentences.quote_normalized: for 4~anyons on the qutrit space (dim~3), all $b_4$ relations verified as $3 \times 3$ matrix identities over $k$, with $\sigma_1 \neq \sigma_3$ (genuine $b_4$, not factoring through $b_3$) and su(3) lie algebra spanning data establishing density in su(3).
sentences.agent_notes: FibonacciQutrit (9 thms) + FibonacciQutritUniversality (10 thms) host B_4 relations on dim-3 qutrit space and su(3) spanning. Fusion-tree calculation gives dim-3 fusion space for 4 Fibonacci anyons.

meta.quote_normalized: for 4~anyons on the qutrit space (dim~3), all $b_4$ relations verified as $3 \times 3$ matrix identities over $k$, with $\sigma_1 \neq \sigma_3$ (genuine $b_4$, not factoring through $b_3$) and su(3) lie algebra spanning data establishing density in su(3).

blocking_issues.summary: Acknowledgments cite Lean v4.28.0 + Mathlib 8f9d9cff; project pins are Lean v4.29.0 + Mathlib 8850ed93. Toolchain pin drift.

Internal arithmetic drift: abstract claims '7 new modules', conclusion claims 'Nine Lean 4 modules'; intro '~60 theorems' and conclusion '~115 theorems' both undercount live grep counts (81-96 and 122-160 respectively across plausible scopes). All are WARN-level under tilde tolerance but should be reconciled before submission.

non_blocking_followups.summary: Citation registry coverage gap: bibitems witten1989, rt1991, bhmv1995, wenzl1988, turaev2010, paper14, paper11 are all present in paper bibliography but absent from CITATION_REGISTRY (src/core/citations.py). Plus inline 'Freedman--Larsen--Wang 2002' (line 174) is referenced without \cite or bibitem.
